# UC-22.56.27 · TSA-SD multi-modal Cybersecurity Operations Centre (CSOC / SOC) tier coverage + on-call attestation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Our cyber-security operations centre must have eyes on the screen 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. We confirm every day that no time slot was left uncovered.*

---

## Description

Surveys SOC tier coverage to confirm 24×7 across T1, T2, T3.

## Value

TSA inspectors expect 24×7 monitoring; gaps in roster are findings.

## Implementation

(1) Wire PagerDuty schedule sync to `tsa_soc_coverage_lookup`. (2) Schedule UC-22.56.27 daily. (3) Build `TSA SOC Coverage` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.56.27` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `tsa_sd_clause="TSA-SD-multi-CSOC-coverage"`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Drop T1 coverage for any 1-hour window in `tsa_soc_coverage_lookup`. UC-22.56.27 MUST flag.

### Negative scenario

Maintain 24x7 T1/T2/T3 coverage. UC-22.56.27 MUST NOT escalate.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk ES + PagerDuty TA.
- KV Store `tsa_soc_coverage_lookup`.

## Step 1 — Coverage register

```ini
[tsa_soc_coverage_lookup]
field.tier = string
field.start = number
field.end = number
field.analyst_id = string
field.mssp = string
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.56.27: TSA SOC Coverage]
cron_schedule = 25 10 * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.56.27,reg=TSA-Surface,clause=TSA-SD-multi-CSOC-coverage
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Runbook

**`coverage_gap=true`**: fill gap within 24 hours; report to CC.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

- **PagerDuty sync lag**: ensure sync runs at least every 6 hours.
- **MSSP feed**: MSSPs may not feed schedule; build manual lookup.

**TSA-specific failure modes**:
- **Sector ISAC analyst integration**: sector-ISAC analyst time may count as virtual coverage; document.
- **Holiday/staffing surges**: pre-plan coverage during industry events.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup tsa_soc_coverage_lookup
| eval coverage_hour_start = strftime(start, "%Y-%m-%dT%H")
| eval coverage_hour_end = strftime(end, "%Y-%m-%dT%H")
| stats values(analyst_id) as analysts by tier coverage_hour_start coverage_hour_end
| eval expected = 1
| stats sum(expected) as covered_hours dc(coverage_hour_start) as unique_hours by tier
| eval coverage_gap = if(unique_hours < 24, "true", "false")
| where coverage_gap="true"
| table tier covered_hours unique_hours coverage_gap
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Tier coverage by hour. (Panel 2) Gap-trend. (Panel 3) Analyst utilisation. (Panel 4) Escalation chain.

## Known False Positives

1. **Cross-tier hand-off**: T1 may yield T2 directly; ensure roster covers union of tiers.
2. **MSSP coverage**: outsourced T1 must be reflected.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `tsa_22_56_27_exceptions`.

## References

- [TSA SD 24x7 monitoring expectation](https://www.tsa.gov/cybersecurity)
- [NIST 800-86 — Computer Forensics](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-86/final)
